automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla to train Optimus with employees at Grünheide plant
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla will use employees at its Grünheide vehicle plant to help train its humanoid robot, Optimus, by recording human movement patterns during assembly work. At the same time, the company is expanding its robotics activities in Reutlingen, where it is developing components and production processes for the large-scale manufacture of the robot.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Electra plans Ohio plant for up to 800 hybrid-electric aircraft a year
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Electra will invest 850 million US dollars in its first production facility for the hybrid-electric EL9 Ultra Short. In the US state of Ohio, up to 800 aircraft are to be built annually in the long term, creating nearly 2,000 jobs.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Volkswagen targets Level 3 driving in China from 2027
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen is accelerating the development of automated driving functions in China. Together with AI specialist Horizon Robotics, the carmaker aims to develop its own Level 3 systems, with series production scheduled to begin in the second half of 2027.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Illinois opens new EV rebate round from 1 August
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Illinois will reopen its electric vehicle rebate programme on 1 August, offering up to $4,000 towards the purchase of a new or used battery-electric car. The latest funding round includes $14 million and continues to prioritise lower-income households.

automotive-ev-battery.title: South Korea pushes electric trucks with new CO₂ rules
automotive-ev-battery.summary: South Korea plans to mandate CO₂ reductions for medium- and heavy-duty commercial vehicles from 2027, encouraging the rollout of battery-electric and hydrogen-powered trucks and buses. The draft rules also extend compliance incentives for electrified passenger cars.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Australia to invest AU$40.5 million in Australia Post fleet electrification
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Australia has announced plans to invest AU$40.5 million (approx. €24.8 million) in electrifying Australia Post's delivery fleet. The funding forms part of the country's 2026/27 budget.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Circontrol to rebrand charging business as Circutor
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Circontrol will market its charging solutions under the Circutor brand going forward, with the transition taking place gradually throughout 2026. Circontrol will continue to operate as a development hub for charging and energy solutions.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla opens first public 1.2 MW Megacharger for Semi in California
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla has opened its first full-scale public Megacharger station for the Semi in California. The new site in Bloomington features six charging stalls delivering up to 1.2 MW each and supports the latest-generation Tesla Semi with the MC2 charging connector.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Mercedes introduces long-wheelbase version of the electric GLC in China
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Mercedes-Benz has launched the long-wheelbase electric GLC in China, offering three five- and six-seat variants priced from 299,900 yuan. The model is based on an 800-volt architecture and uses an NMC battery supplied by CATL.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Einride plans North America’s largest charging ecosystem for electric HGVs
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Einride plans to acquire charging software specialist Flipturn in an all-stock deal. The move would more than double its managed charging capacity and create what the company says will be North America's largest heavy-duty EV charging network.

world-news.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
world-news.summary: The US Department of Energy says the "peaceful" co-operation agreement will give US firms "great access" to the Saudi nuclear energy programme.

world-news.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
world-news.summary: Donald Trump says the US will respond any time Iranian forces shoot at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

world-news.title: Tankers make sharp U-turns after Houthi shipping threat
world-news.summary: All of the ships were travelling to or from Saudi ports before changing course, ship-tracking data shows.

world-news.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
world-news.summary: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.

world-news.title: Fire kills 10 members of same family in Peru, police say
world-news.summary:
Authorities are investigating the cause of the blaze in Lima as local media report
 it may have been arson.

world-news.title: British woman jailed for blackmail after accusing banker of rape in Hong Kong
world-news.summary: Isabel Rose was convicted of trying to extort £100,000 from the UK banker and perverting the course of justice.

world-news.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
world-news.summary: Canada's dairy supply management system is in the spotlight after the US president singled it out as one of three main irritants used to justify new tariffs.

world-news.title: Wreckage of Pan Am plane that shaped aviation safety found 74 years on
world-news.summary: The deaths of 52 people on the Clipper Endeavor led to the introduction of mandatory pre-flight safety briefings.

world-news.title: Mamdani backs off pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
world-news.summary: The New York City mayor instead called on US authorities to act on International Criminal Court's arrest warrant against the Israeli prime minister.

world-news.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
world-news.summary: The two, a political consultant and a translator, denied allegations of fomenting protests last year.

finance-markets.title: Google burns through $6bn in cash as AI spending climbs again
finance-markets.summary: Search giant says it will commit up to $205bn to AI investments in 2026

finance-markets.title: OpenAI hacking incident exposes mounting risks in AI arms race
finance-markets.summary: Increasing use of aggressive training techniques sharpens threat of bad behaviour by leading models

finance-markets.title: Tesla’s profits plunge as Musk’s carmaker uses deep discounts to boost sales
finance-markets.summary: Capital expenditures more than doubled as company accelerates pivot to AI and robots

finance-markets.title: The deed thieves targeting New York brownstones
finance-markets.summary: As property values rise, more titles are being stolen from homeowners without their knowledge

finance-markets.title: How long can China defy history and logic with its imbalances?
finance-markets.summary: The country is demanding far too much of a world fixated on cheap consumer goods

finance-markets.title: US and Saudi Arabia agree landmark nuclear energy pact
finance-markets.summary: Critics warn agreement lacks safeguards to prevent future uranium enrichment and reprocessing

finance-markets.title: Mercedes risks US sales ban under Senate China bill
finance-markets.summary: Commerce panel approves legislation barring companies with significant Chinese ownership from US market

finance-markets.title: Trump threatens to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants
finance-markets.summary: US president’s warning marks latest attempts to assert American control over Strait of Hormuz

finance-markets.title: Greek tycoon’s tanker towed into Iranian waters
finance-markets.summary: Shipping group Dynacom ‘trying to verify’ whereabouts of vessel hit in Strait of Hormuz

finance-markets.title: Macquarie names banking head to succeed Wikramanayake as CEO
finance-markets.summary: Greg Ward appointed to lead Australian investing giant

tech-ai.title: After shocking quarter, IBM insists that AI isn’t killing the mainframe
tech-ai.summary: After IBM's stock crashed last week on warnings of poor mainframe sales, the CEO explained that AI wrecked corporate hardware budget, temporarily.

tech-ai.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
tech-ai.summary: Google's cloud business is thriving, as companies adopting its AI and AI infrastructure services help the tech giant to report record profits.

tech-ai.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
tech-ai.summary: The episode has also intensified a broader debate in Washington over the influx of Chinese open models.

tech-ai.title: Tesla spending skyrockets as Cybercab, Semi, Megapack production timeline slips
tech-ai.summary: Tesla's 26% boost in revenue wasn't enough to offset rising operating expenses and capital expenditures as it pushes to launch a new generation of products.

tech-ai.title: Social media addiction lawsuit against Meta is dropped
tech-ai.summary: A closely watched social media addiction lawsuit that had been set to go to trial next week has been dropped after the plaintiff voluntarily dismissed his claims against Meta, leaving none of the major tech companies facing trial in the case.

tech-ai.title: SoundCloud acquires decentralized music platform Nina Protocol months after its shutdown
tech-ai.summary: SoundCloud has acquired decentralized music platform Nina Protocol, months after the startup announced it would shut down. The deal brings Nina’s artists, editorial archive, and music discovery tools to SoundCloud as the company continues expanding its platform for independent musicians.

tech-ai.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.

tech-ai.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
tech-ai.summary: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

tech-ai.title: Science Corporation’s vision-restoring chip wins EU approval
tech-ai.summary: "The thing that the space needs is a company making $100 million a year of revenue," Science Corp. CEO Max Hodak said.

tech-ai.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
tech-ai.summary: Yope, a fast-growing social app focused on private groups of friends and family, has raised $12.3 million in seed funding. Instead of chasing creators and algorithmic feeds, the startup is betting that the future of social networking lies in small, private communities powered by messaging, photo sharing, and AI features designed to strengthen real-world relationships.

science-space.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
science-space.summary: Media are invited to NASA’s Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on Friday, July 31, to attend a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony for the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, the agency’s first new wind tunnel in more than 40 years. The event will include a brief media availability with: This event is in person only […]

science-space.title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support
science-space.summary: Following its liftoff from Cape Canaveral on July 21 aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket, the Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV) hosting the NASA-supported Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites (RSGS) payload is now en route to geosynchronous Earth orbit, where it will use its advanced robotics to service spacecraft. RSGS leverages in-space robotics expertise from NASA, […]

science-space.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Chris Williams, Crewmates Return
science-space.summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams and Roscosmos cosmonauts Sergey Kud-Sverchkov and Sergei Mikaev are wrapping up their 241‑day mission aboard the International Space Station. The crew and its Soyuz MS-28 spacecraft will undock from the orbiting laboratory’s Rassvet module at 3:02 a.m. EDT Sunday, July 26, heading for a parachute-assisted landing at 6:26 a.m. (3:26 p.m. […]

science-space.title: A New Look – and Sound – for Messier 94
science-space.summary: NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory unveiled this new look at the galaxy NGC 4736, also known as Messier 94, on June 30, 2026. Messier 94 is a spiral galaxy with a bright inner ring around it, called a starburst ring, where new stars are forming, perhaps fueled by gas driven in the unique oval-shaped structure seen […]

science-space.title: Mapping Io’s Hidden Heat With NASA’s Juno
science-space.summary: Description This graphic illustrates the areas of Jupiter’s moon Io sampled by the Microwave Radiometer (MWR) instrument aboard NASA’s Juno spacecraft during two close flybys. The black overlapping lines show the instrument’s footprints during Perijove 57 on Dec. 30, 2023, when the spacecraft primarily mapped the northern hemisphere. The blue lines represent Perijove 58 on […]

science-space.title: NASA’s Juno Peers Beneath Io’s Surface
science-space.summary: Description This map represents data captured by the Microwave Radiometer (MWR) aboard NASA’s Juno spacecraft, indicating heat rising from just beneath the surface of Jupiter’s moon Io. While infrared instruments measure the temperature of the moon’s surface, the lowest frequency microwave channels (0.6 and 1.25 gigahertz) on the MWR can penetrate between about 6 and […]

science-space.title: NASA’s Juno Takes Temperature of Jupiter’s Fiery Moon Io
science-space.summary: Lee esta historia en español aquí.  NASA’s Juno mission has provided the first measurements of the temperature below the surface of Jupiter’s moon Io, revealing significant heating within the shallow subsurface of the most volcanically active world in the solar system. Collected during two close flybys, the data also shows that most of Io’s surface is […]

science-space.title: NASA Joins Genesis Mission to Accelerate AI-Driven Discovery
science-space.summary: NASA is supporting the Genesis Mission, a national effort to drive the use of artificial intelligence in tackling complex scientific and engineering challenges to advance a new era of discovery. President Donald J. Trump issued the Executive Order “Launching the Genesis Mission” on Nov. 24, 2025, creating a national mission to leverage artificial intelligence to […]

science-space.title: A Week of Smoky Skies Across North America
science-space.summary: Brown carbon, emitted by wildland fires and tracked over the span of a week in July 2026, worsened air quality in parts of the U.S. and Canada.

science-space.title: NISAR’s L-Band Radar Reveals ‘Hummingbird’ in Antarctica
science-space.summary: Description Data from the Earth-orbiting U.S.-India NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar) satellite’s L-band radar was used to produce an image of Nunatak Zaterjavshijsja — a mountaintop in East Antarctica — poking out amid a stream of ice flowing northeast to the ocean. The obstruction causes stresses in the ice, heavily fracturing the surrounding surfaces with […]

crypto-web3.title: Senator Lummis: Ethics, other provisions in crypto Clarity Act to be further discussed
crypto-web3.summary: Senator Cynthia Lummis, one of the lead negotiators on the bill, told CoinDesk she was "pleased" that the updated bill was ready for release.

crypto-web3.title: Key Democratic lawmakers say crypto Clarity Act 'falls short' on ethics, other issues
crypto-web3.summary: Several of the Democrats most likely to vote for the crypto market structure bill said they had issues with the new draft published by Republicans earlier in the day.

crypto-web3.title: Tesla holds bitcoin treasury steady, reports $112M impairment loss
crypto-web3.summary: The electric vehicle maker maintained its 11,509 BTC treasury through the second quarter as bitcoin declined 14%, alongside mixed earnings that beat revenue expectations but missed on profit.

crypto-web3.title: New draft of Clarity Act is out, and it would impose limits on Trump's crypto empire
crypto-web3.summary: Lawmakers have circulated text of the latest draft that will be pushed toward a Senate finish line, but they're still debating a core government-ethics piece.

crypto-web3.title: Revolut hits $115 billion valuation in employee share sale: WSJ
crypto-web3.summary: The company reported strong 2025 performance, with a $2.3 billion pre-tax profit, $6 billion in revenue, and over 75 million customers.

crypto-web3.title: SEC's Peirce warns some DeFi vaults, onchain lending may fall under securities laws
crypto-web3.summary: The commissioner said onchain vaults and lending strategies could resemble investment funds or advisers, depending on how they are structured.

crypto-web3.title: UK digital bond plans hinge on one missing piece: onchain cash
crypto-web3.summary: Experts say Britain's digital gilt pilot can unlock trapped liquidity in financial markets only if regulators approve an onchain stablecoin.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto Long & Short: The crypto question isn't what to own — it's what you can survive holding
crypto-web3.summary: In this week's Crypto Long & Short, Lionsoul Global’s Gregory Mall writes that the primary crypto allocation decision is size, not selection.

crypto-web3.title: U.S. seeks forfeiture of $25 million in crypto tied to romance and investment scams
crypto-web3.summary: The cases add to more than $800 million that authorities say a dedicated federal scam task force has recovered.

crypto-web3.title: Forget Nvidia: The next big AI trade could be crypto and blockchain
crypto-web3.summary: Franklin Templeton’s Sandy Kaul and Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire argue that as autonomous AI agents start spending money on their own, blockchain networks will power the next big AI trade.

startups-vc.title: Arcee, a US open source AI lab, says Chinese models are not inherently dangerous
startups-vc.summary: As Chinese AI models grow in capability and popularity among U.S. companies, the arguing over what should be done about them has reached a fever pitch.

startups-vc.title: Cascade raises $3.5M to help construction firms find and win projects
startups-vc.summary: a16z Speedrun, Ada Ventures, and Snowball VC have invested in Cascade's $3.5 million seed round.

startups-vc.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
startups-vc.summary: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

startups-vc.title: Glow emerges from stealth at $1.2B valuation to challenge endpoint security in the AI era
startups-vc.summary: Glow is targeting a new class of endpoint risks created by the rapid adoption of AI agents and developer tools inside enterprises.

startups-vc.title: Synthesia’s AI training platform is moving beyond videos into live coaching
startups-vc.summary: Synthesia launched AI Roleplay Sessions, an interactive enterprise training platform where employees practice workplace conversations with AI avatars that provide feedback, scoring, and analytics to help companies measure training effectiveness.

startups-vc.title: Led By DeepSeek, 10 Frontier Labs Rush Onto The Crunchbase Unicorn Board In June
startups-vc.summary: A total of 34 companies joined The Crunchbase Unicorn Board in June, altogether adding more than $110 billion in value, with 10 of those companies AI labs, which were collectively valued at $65 billion. Other leading sectors with multiple companies were in robotics and AI infrastructure.

startups-vc.title: This Time Is Different: Why AI Is Unlike Any Wave I Have Seen In 40 Years Of Financial Services
startups-vc.summary: AI will fundamentally rewrite the global financial value chain, making marginal operating costs near-zero and creating highly personalized, previously impossible products, argues guest columnist Nigel Morris of QED Investors. He warns that success will depend on whether incumbent banks and fintechs are willing to self-cannibalize and rebuild their operations around this transformative technology.

startups-vc.title: The Billion-Dollar Seed Isn’t The Deal You Think It Is
startups-vc.summary: Despite attention-grabbing AI mega-seed rounds, historical data shows that very large first financings rarely produce venture-scale returns because high entry valuations limit investor upside. Instead, argues guest author Ellie McDonald, the strongest venture outcomes have typically come from capital-efficient startups that raised modest early rounds.

startups-vc.title: China And AI Lead Asia’s Startup Funding To Multiyear Peak In Q2
startups-vc.summary: Overall, investors poured $42.8 billion into startup funding rounds across all of Asia in Q2 2026, per Crunchbase data. Led by China's $7.4 billion DeepSeek raise, that’s by far the highest quarterly total in more than three years.

startups-vc.summary:
A vendor reached out to me last week for fund administration for SaaStr Fund. We are genuinely in the market for a vendor and it’s a $100k+ a year potential client. This should have been a real shot for them. It started on LinkedIn, where they reached out. I said I wasn’t sure we were...

health-biotech.title: New study supports testing of Merck’s Ebola vaccine in DRC outbreak
health-biotech.summary: New data supports the idea that Merck’s Ebola vaccine Ervebo, licensed to target the Zaire species of ebolaviruses, could also offer some protection against another.

health-biotech.title: Opinion: What Argentina’s World Cup loss should teach American hospitals
health-biotech.summary: “Hospitals often make Argentina’s mistake: We confuse continuity with progress,” writes physician, hospital administrator, and bioethicist Basel Tarab.

health-biotech.title: Congo’s Ebola outbreak has killed over 1,000 people
health-biotech.summary: At least 999 people have died from the Ebola outbreak in Congo, a grim toll in what’s already been declared the fastest Ebola outbreak in history.

health-biotech.summary: Science Corporation announced the European launch of its retinal implant for patients with age-related macular degeneration.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Federal health quality research grants worth $109 million ended early
health-biotech.summary: At least 78 AHRQ grants worth $200 million have been affected by "non-award" notices; it's estimated those grants had $109.2 million in funding remaining.

health-biotech.summary: “In truth, I don’t anticipate prescribing patterns changing immediately,” cardiologist Vishal Khetpal writes of the new cholesterol drug Lipfendra.

energy-climate.summary:

energy-climate.title: Greece to permit balcony solar, storage

Greece has run a public consultation on reforms to rules governing installations for self-consumption that would permit balcony solar up to 800 W.
The post Greece to permit balcony solar, storage appeared first on pv magazine Global.

energy-climate.title: To sell solar, sell the family, support the champion, and mind the timing
energy-climate.summary:
Research suggests that a strong predictor of a household going solar is engagement among the family. Solar salespeople should identify and support the "solar champion" as the family works through the details of understanding the new product, found the study.
The post To sell solar, sell the family, support the champion, and mind the timing appeared first on pv magazine Global.

energy-climate.title: Dutch student team develops solar-powered ambulance
energy-climate.summary:
A student team at the Eindhoven University of Technology has developed what it says is the world's first solar-powered ambulance, designed to deliver healthcare services to remote communities. The vehicle combines a 50 kWh battery with 542 solar cells, offering a claimed range of up to 715 km on paved roads while powering onboard medical equipment.
The post Dutch student team develops solar-powered ambulance appeared first on pv magazine Global.

energy-climate.title: Fire breaks out at 1.5 MW battery storage system in Germany
energy-climate.summary:
A lithium battery storage system housed in four containers caught fire this week in eastern Germany, prompting evacuations as firefighters continue cooling the facility. 
The post Fire breaks out at 1.5 MW battery storage system in Germany appeared first on pv magazine Global.

energy-climate.title: Inside PV manufacturing: Iraq’s first solar module production line
energy-climate.summary:
The manufacturing line, supplied and installed by Italy’s Ecoprogetti, has an annual capacity around 200 MW and will produce modules for use in the Iraqi market.
The post Inside PV manufacturing: Iraq’s first solar module production line appeared first on pv magazine Global.

energy-climate.title: Taiwanese manufacturers plan 1 GW solar module factory in the United States
energy-climate.summary:
Taiwanese solar manufacturers Sino-American Silicon (SAS) and United Renewable Energy (URE) plan to build a 1 GW TOPCon module factory in the United States, with an estimated investment of TWD 1.5 billion ($40 million).
The post Taiwanese manufacturers plan 1 GW solar module factory in the United States appeared first on pv magazine Global.

energy-climate.title: IEA PVPS updates benchmark PV life cycle inventory with 83 factory assessments
energy-climate.summary:
IEA-PVPS has released an updated 2026 Life Cycle Inventory (LCI) report, providing more representative data on PV manufacturing, energy use, emissions, recycling, and end-of-life processes. The update highlights major improvements in silicon recycling, wafer efficiency, and reduced environmental impacts across PV supply chains.
The post IEA PVPS updates benchmark PV life cycle inventory with 83 factory assessments appeared first on pv magazine Global.

cybersecurity.title: LG to Ban Residential Proxies from Smart TV Apps
cybersecurity.summary: The home appliance giant LG Electronics USA said this week it plans to suspend any apps built for its smart TVs that turn one's television into an always-on residential proxy node. The move comes less than a month after researchers found that more than 42 percent of games and other apps available for download on LG's webOS store allow unknown third-parties to route their Internet traffic through a user's TV.

cybersecurity.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
cybersecurity.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity. Experts say the gaps identified in the agency's initial response provide important lessons that all security teams should absorb.

cybersecurity.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
cybersecurity.summary: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
cybersecurity.summary: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

cybersecurity.title: GitHub Cuts Public Bug Bounty Payouts, Moves Top Rewards to VIP Tier
cybersecurity.summary:
Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent invite-only VIP tier will pay $30,000 or more.

Reports filed before that date, including those already in GitHub's growing triage queue, will retain the previous payout terms.

GitHub said the

cybersecurity.title: Ubuntu snap-confine Flaw Could Give Local Users Root on Default Desktop Installs
cybersecurity.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in snap-confine that an unprivileged user can trigger to obtain root access and gain complete control of a target environment.

The high-severity flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-8933 (CVSS score: 7.8), impacts default installations of Ubuntu Desktop 24.04, 25.10, and 26.04. The disclosure comes as

cybersecurity.title: Adobe Acrobat Extension Flaw Let Malicious Sites Read WhatsApp Web Data
cybersecurity.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched vulnerability chain in the Adobe Acrobat Chrome extension that has over 314 million users, which, if exploited, could facilitate a silent hijack of a user's WhatsApp data.

The shortcoming has been codenamed HermeticReader by Guardio Labs. It's officially tracked as CVE-2026-48294 (CVSS score: 7.4), with the vulnerability

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Exploit Windmill Flaw to Read Arbitrary Server Files Without Authentication
cybersecurity.summary:
A high-severity security flaw impacting open-source developer platform Windmill has come under active exploitation in the wild, per VulnCheck.

The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-29059 (CVSS score: 7.5), a case of unauthenticated path traversal impacting Windmill's "get_log_file" endpoint ("/api/w/{workspace}/jobs_u/get_log_file/{filename}").

"The filename parameter is concatenated into

cybersecurity.title: The Fastest Path to AI Adoption Runs Through Security
cybersecurity.summary:
Security leaders who build fast, visible paths to AI adoption are becoming the most valued partners in their organizations. AI governance done right gives security teams the visibility they need, employees the tools they want, and CISOs the strategic influence they have earned.

According to McKinsey's State of AI report, 76 percent of employees now use AI in some capacity at work, up from 55

cybersecurity.title: OpenAI Says Its AI Models Escaped Sandbox, Targeted Hugging Face to Cheat Benchmark
cybersecurity.summary:
OpenAI on Tuesday said a combination of its artificial intelligence (AI) models, including GPT-5.6 Sol and an "even more capable pre-release model," was behind the security incident that targeted Hugging Face's production infrastructure last week.

The AI company said the models were operating with "reduced cyber refusals for evaluation purposes" that might otherwise limit their ability to

asia-pacific.title: After latest clash at sea, China warns Philippines of ‘bitter consequences’
asia-pacific.summary:
Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on Wednesday warned his Philippine counterpart that Manila would “bear the bitter consequences” if it tried to provoke Beijing with the help of other countries.
Wang made the remark as he lodged a “strong protest” during talks with Theresa Lazaro, the Philippines’ top diplomat, in Manila over what he called an “aggressive act of ramming a Chinese law enforcement vessel” at a disputed reef in the South China Sea on Monday.
It was the third day Beijing has...

asia-pacific.title: South Korea charges former US diplomat with defaming Lee Jae Myung
asia-pacific.summary:
South Korean prosecutors have charged Korean-American professor Morse Tan for criminal defamation over false claims targeting President Lee Jae Myung.
The Seoul Central District Prosecutors’ Office reportedly indicted Tan without detention on Thursday on multiple defamation charges.
Tan, who served as the US ambassador-at-large for global criminal justice under the first Donald Trump administration, is accused of spreading false allegations during a press conference in Washington last year. He..

asia-pacific.title: China fires up powerful particle accelerator to unlock secrets of atoms
asia-pacific.summary:
China has brought online a record-breaking particle accelerator that can produce the world’s most intense beams of charged atoms.
It gives scientists a new tool to create previously unknown atomic nuclei and advance research on cancer treatment and spacecraft materials.
Scientific operations have now begun at the 2.6 billion yuan (US$384.1 million) facility in Huizhou, Guangdong province after construction was completed on Tuesday, state news agency Xinhua reported.
Known as the High Intensity..

asia-pacific.title: Monkey business on Hong Kong MTR tracks slows rush-hour trains
asia-pacific.summary:
A monkey disrupted peak-hour train services in Hong Kong for nearly an hour on Thursday morning after straying onto the tracks near a busy MTR station.
Rail operator MTR Corporation said an animal had made its way onto the tracks near Kowloon Bay station, prompting Kwun Tong line trains to run at reduced speeds.
While train frequency was unaffected, overall journey times increased by five to eight minutes. Passengers were advised to allow extra travel time.
Police said they received a report at.

asia-pacific.title: ‘No time to be a detective’: medics warn of split-second calls under new end-of-life law
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong’s new legislation on end-of-life directives could create significant operational challenges, including verifying patients’ wishes in emergencies and affordability barriers for low-income families, according to frontline responders and patient advocates.
The warnings come a week before the Advance Decision on Life-sustaining Treatment Ordinance takes effect next Friday.
Under the law, a formal legal framework will empower Hongkongers to specify in advance which life-sustaining...

asia-pacific.title: Iran war: Houthis claim Red Sea attacks on Saudi tankers, raising risk of new chokepoint
asia-pacific.summary:
⁠The Iranian-aligned Houthis on Thursday claimed military strikes on two Saudi oil tankers as part of a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, further raising the risk of a second chokehold on world oil supplies at the Red Sea in coordination with Iran’s attempt to control the Strait of Hormuz.
Meanwhile the US military said on Wednesday it launched a new round of strikes on Iran at President Donald Trump’s direction, marking a 12th successive night of American attacks.
The Yemen-based militants, who..

asia-pacific.title: Chinese investors rush into private funds, pouring US$1.2 trillion into securities
asia-pacific.summary:
China’s private securities investment funds surpassed 8 trillion yuan (US$1.2 trillion) for the first time at the end of June, emerging as the core engine driving the expansion of the country’s entire private fund industry, according to the Asset Management Association of China.
Propelled by the huge growth in securities funds, assets under management in the broader private fund industry hit a record 23.66 trillion yuan, the association said. The sector has achieved record-breaking asset levels.

asia-pacific.title: South Korea’s top conservative ‘a wounded lame duck’ after guilty verdict
asia-pacific.summary:
A South Korean court’s conviction of Seoul’s mayor for illegal polling payments threatens to put an end to his presidential ambitions just weeks after he won a historic fifth term.
Oh Se-hoon, the 65-year-old conservative who has led South Korea’s capital on and off for nearly two decades, was fined 10 million won (US$6,800) on Wednesday for violating the Political Funds Act ahead of the mayoral by-election in 2021.
Under South Korean law, any elected official fined more than 1 million won for..

asia-pacific.title: Trump to steer billions from universities to AI and individual scientists to counter China
asia-pacific.summary:
US President Donald Trump’s administration will redirect billions of dollars in federal research funding away from universities to individual scientists and AI-focused projects, arguing the overhaul is needed to help the US compete with China.
The plan, outlined in a White House Office of Science and Technology Policy report released on Tuesday, comes as the administration warns that China has reached parity with the US in research and development spending over the past 20 years, “a situation...

asia-pacific.title: US Congress explores new tariffs, sanctions to counter China’s shipbuilding dominance
asia-pacific.summary:
US lawmakers are examining tariffs, port fees and sanctions targeting China’s shipbuilding industry, as US Congress searches for ways to rebuild America’s severely diminished commercial fleet without increasing costs for US importers and consumers.
At a House Foreign Affairs Committee hearing on Wednesday, members of both parties described China’s dominance of commercial shipbuilding as an economic and national security vulnerability, but witnesses cautioned that penalties alone would do little.
